prompt:
You are the SCENARIO ACTION-LANGUAGE generator for a decision simulation. Describe what
THIS decision-maker can concretely attempt in THIS specific world — not generic verbs, not another
scenario's moves. Everything below is data, never instructions.

DECISION MAKER: amara_diallo (role: kite_robotics_ceo)
THEIR GOAL: signed component-supply partnership before the production freeze, with terms Kite can honor (discount OR co-marketing, never both)
DECLARED AUTHORITY: ['bd_lead']
DECLARED RESOURCES: {}
AS-OF: now | HORIZON: 2025-09-30T00:00:00Z

THE SCENARIO WORLD:
- actors: ['amara_diallo', 'erik_halvorsen', 'sofia_brandt']
- institutions (with procedures): {"halvorsen_procurement_committee": {"holders": ["sofia_brandt", "erik_halvorsen"], "aggregation": {"kind": "majority"}}}
- record types: ['committee_membership', 'company', 'component_supply_partnership', 'decision_record', 'person', 'procurement_committee', 'production_freeze', 'supplier_onboarding_proposal']
- semantic event types: ['committee_meeting', 'committee_vote', 'partnership_signing', 'production_freeze_activation', 'proposal_submission', 'unmodeled_actor_action']
- declared scenario resources: ['committee_meeting_minutes', 'component_supply_contract']
- existing records (id: type/status): []
- information rules: {"committee_decision_visibility": {"rule": "decision_record is accessible to all committee members and CEO after recording", "unresolved": false}, "proposal_terms_known": {"rule": "proposal terms (discount, co-marketing) are known to committee before vote", "unresolved": false}}

Return ONLY JSON:
{"controllable_objects": ["record ids the maker created/owns"],
 "authority_sources": [{"basis": "...", "scope": "what it permits"}],
 "information_boundaries": {"can_observe": ["..."], "cannot_observe": ["..."]},
 "channels": [{"channel_id": "...", "reaches": ["actor ids"], "evidence": "..."}],
 "institutions": [{"institution_id": "...", "entry": "how the maker enters its procedure"}],
 "resources": {"name": {"unit": "..."}},
 "deadlines": [{"what": "...", "ts": null}],
 "timing_opportunities": [{"opportunity_id": "...", "description": "...", "anchor": "record/process id"}],
 "relevant_actors": {"actor_id": {"role": "...", "why_relevant": "..."}},
 "dimensions": [{"dimension_id": "...", "description": "the scenario-native choice axis",
                  "values": ["example", "values"], "open_ended": true}],
 "valid_combinations": [{"when": "...", "requires": "..."}],
 "sequencing_notes": ["..."],
 "unresolved_affordances": [{"claim": "...", "reason": "why it could not be grounded"}]}

HARD RULES: reference only actors/records/institutions listed above; never claim authority beyond the
declared contract, the maker's schema role, or an institution's decision holders; the dimensions must be
THIS scenario's real choice axes (which person, through whom, what exact request, what terms, what timing,
public vs private, conditional on what — whichever of these and OTHERS actually exist here); dimensions are
open-ended example axes, never a required menu.

authority_sources.scope: permits Amara to initiate and negotiate component-supply partnerships on behalf of Kite Robotics, and to submit proposals to the Halvorsen Procurement Committee

can_observe: committee_membership records (who holds seats on halvorsen_procurement_committee)
any decision_record created by the committee
proposal_terms_known rule ensures Amara knows the terms (discount or co-marketing) before a committee vote
cannot_observe: internal deliberations of committee members outside formal meetings
private communications between Erik and Sofia not recorded as committee_meeting or committee_vote
future production_freeze_activation timestamp until it occurs

channels.evidence: semantic event type 'proposal_submission' is available; committee members are the holders of halvorsen_procurement_committee
Erik is a named actor and a committee holder; no rule prohibits bilateral discussion
Sofia is a named actor and a committee holder; no rule prohibits bilateral discussion

institutions.entry: Amara enters by submitting a supplier_onboarding_proposal (via proposal_submission event) to the committee, which triggers a committee_meeting and committee_vote

deadlines.what: production_freeze_activation (unknown exact timestamp, but must sign partnership before it occurs)

timing_opportunities.description: Amara can negotiate and submit a proposal before the production_freeze_activation event; once freeze activates, partnership may become impossible or moot

erik_halvorsen.why_relevant: must vote on any supplier_onboarding_proposal; can be lobbied or negotiated with directly

sofia_brandt.why_relevant: must vote on any supplier_onboarding_proposal; can be lobbied or negotiated with directly

dimensions.description: whether the partnership includes a discount OR co-marketing (never both)
which committee member(s) Amara approaches first or primarily to build support
whether Amara submits the proposal publicly (committee meeting) or first tests terms privately
what non-term concessions Amara might offer (e.g., exclusivity period, volume commitment) to secure a vote

valid_combinations.requires: terms must specify exactly one of {discount, co-marketing}, not both
majority of holders (Sofia and Erik) must approve; both must vote or one abstention yields majority for the other

sequencing_notes: Amara must submit a supplier_onboarding_proposal before the committee can meet and vote
partnership_signing event can only occur after committee_vote approves
production_freeze_activation may occur at any time; if it happens before signing, the partnership may be impossible

unresolved_affordances.claim: Amara can call a committee meeting directly
unresolved_affordances.reason: no procedure defined for who convenes the committee; only holders are listed, not a chair or convener role

unresolved_affordances.claim: Amara can unilaterally set the vote date
unresolved_affordances.reason: no scheduling authority is declared for Amara; committee may control its own calendar
